cost_driver: RAM allocated or consumed by the n8n process
applies_to: PaaS, Cloud Run, AWS Fargate, VPS
how_it_surprises: Large payloads, binary data, and concurrent executions can force a bigger instance or higher memory allocation
control: Set concurrency limits, move binary data out of memory, split workflows, monitor memory high-water mark
evidence_required: memory chart, execution size, binary data mode

cost_driver: vCPU allocation, CPU seconds, or burst CPU
applies_to: PaaS, Cloud Run, AWS Fargate, VPS
how_it_surprises: Code nodes, transformations, PDF/image work, and high concurrency can increase runtime cost or require larger compute
control: Schedule heavy jobs, use queue workers only after Week 16 gates, cap autoscaling
evidence_required: CPU chart, slow workflow list, queue backlog

cost_driver: Managed PostgreSQL instance size or database service tier
applies_to: PaaS, AWS RDS, Cloud SQL, self-managed Postgres
how_it_surprises: The app may be cheap while the database tier becomes the real baseline cost
control: Right-size DB, monitor connections, use connection pool, review slow queries
evidence_required: DB instance tier, connection count, readiness history

cost_driver: Execution data, workflow metadata, credential metadata, indexes
applies_to: All production deployments
how_it_surprises: Saved success executions and binary metadata grow quietly until storage or performance degrades
control: execution pruning, retention policy, storage alerts, restore drill
evidence_required: DB storage graph, pruning settings, backup size

cost_driver: Database backups, volume snapshots, object versioning
applies_to: VPS, PaaS, AWS, GCP
how_it_surprises: Backups are often omitted in cheap estimates but required for production RPO/RTO
control: Define retention windows, test restore, separate hot and archive backups
evidence_required: backup schedule, restore proof, retention policy

cost_driver: External storage for binary data and generated files
applies_to: AWS S3, GCS, PaaS add-ons, n8n external storage
how_it_surprises: Attachments and workflow-generated files can grow independently of DB size
control: Lifecycle rules, file size policy, storage alerts
evidence_required: bucket size, lifecycle policy, object count

cost_driver: Outbound bandwidth to users, third-party APIs, or cross-region services
applies_to: Cloud Run, AWS, Fly, Render, Railway, VPS
how_it_surprises: Large webhook responses, binary downloads, or cross-region traffic can make a small app expensive
control: Avoid cross-region app/DB, cap response size, budget alerts, direct-to-object-storage links
evidence_required: egress graph, region map, large response audit

cost_driver: Log ingestion, retention, and search
applies_to: AWS CloudWatch, GCP Logging, PaaS logs, centralized logging
how_it_surprises: Debug logs left on in production create log cost and noise
control: Use info level by default, short debug windows, retention rules, structured JSON logs
evidence_required: log volume, retention days, log level

cost_driver: Monitoring service and alerting volume
applies_to: Production PaaS, AWS, GCP, self-hosted Prometheus
how_it_surprises: More workers and services create more metrics and alert paths
control: Monitor only actionable signals, define owner and severity
evidence_required: alert list, dashboard, owner map

cost_driver: Redis service, memory, persistence, worker connectivity
applies_to: Queue mode deployments
how_it_surprises: Adding workers creates a Redis baseline cost and can force DB connection upgrades
control: Use queue mode only after Week 16 gates, monitor Redis memory and queue metrics
evidence_required: Redis plan, queue metrics, worker count

cost_driver: Additional worker processes and their CPU/RAM
applies_to: Queue mode, AWS Fargate, Cloud Run jobs, PaaS workers
how_it_surprises: Horizontal workers increase compute and DB pressure together
control: Set worker concurrency, DB pool, max replicas, autoscaling limits
evidence_required: worker logs, DB connections, queue backlog

cost_driver: Scale-to-zero, max instances, or horizontal replicas
applies_to: Cloud Run, AWS, Fly, Render, Railway
how_it_surprises: Autoscaling can protect availability but multiply compute, DB, logs, and egress cost
control: Set max instances, budget alerts, load test before raising limits
evidence_required: max instance setting, budget alert, load-test result

cost_driver: Volume or disk attached to app or database
applies_to: VPS, Render, Fly, self-managed DB
how_it_surprises: A cheap app plan may not include durable disk or disk expansion can be manual
control: Use managed DB where possible, monitor disk, document volume restore
evidence_required: disk size, mount path, snapshot proof

cost_driver: Load balancer, public IPv4, TLS endpoint, static IP
applies_to: AWS, GCP, VPS, PaaS
how_it_surprises: Small apps can pay nontrivial baseline for LB or public network resources
control: Use simpler managed endpoint for small cases, share only where isolation remains safe
evidence_required: LB config, certificate, DNS record

cost_driver: Vendor support, paid SLA, enterprise features
applies_to: n8n Cloud, AWS, GCP, PaaS
how_it_surprises: Support expectations may be promised to clients without being priced
control: Match client SLA to paid support and maintenance contract
evidence_required: support plan, SLA, client contract

cost_driver: Patch, backup, incident response, security review
applies_to: All self-hosted and semi-managed routes
how_it_surprises: Human time can exceed infrastructure bill, especially for agencies and freelancers
control: Retainer, update windows, incident note discipline, standard blueprint
evidence_required: hours log, patch calendar, incident notes

cost_driver: SSO, audit logs, private networking, secrets management
applies_to: Agency and production deployments
how_it_surprises: Compliance requirements can force enterprise plans or hyperscaler architecture
control: Decide compliance boundary before platform choice
evidence_required: security requirements, audit needs, secret inventory

cost_driver: Platform-specific deploy model, databases, secrets, networking
applies_to: PaaS, AWS, GCP, n8n Cloud
how_it_surprises: Migration later can require DNS, env, DB, storage, credentials, and OAuth changes
control: Keep workflow exports, DB backups, env inventory, migration runbook
evidence_required: export date, backup date, env inventory

cost_driver: Per-client instance, DB, credentials, domains, logs
applies_to: Agency deployments
how_it_surprises: Saving money by sharing instances creates privacy and blast-radius risk
control: One client per instance or strict tenant boundary, separate backups and keys
evidence_required: client map, instance id, DB id, encryption key owner

cost_driver: Free service sleep, low resources, usage caps, deleted inactive resources
applies_to: Render, Railway, Fly, Cloud free tiers
how_it_surprises: Free tiers are learning tools; production webhooks may sleep, throttle, or hit caps
control: Use paid plans for production, define uptime and support expectations
evidence_required: plan name, sleep behavior, uptime proof
